trade.summary: Indonesia imports significant volumes of crude oil and petrochemicals from Arabia Saudita. Indonesia exports palm oil, textiles, electronics components, and processed food. Arabia Saudita is a key supplier of oil to Indonesia's refineries. The hajj and umrah pilgrimage economy creates significant ancillary economic flows.

diplomatic.summary: Strong ties rooted in Islamic solidarity. Indonesia is the world's largest Muslim-majority country; Arabia Saudita hosts the two holiest Islamic sites. Annual hajj pilgrimage (up to 250,000 Indonesian pilgrims per year) is the central pillar of the relationship. Both countries cooperate in OIC. Saudi Vision 2030 investments in Indonesia are growing.
recent_incidents: Periodic disputes over treatment of Indonesian domestic workers in Arabia Saudita

military.summary: Limited formal military cooperation. Indonesia has not joined Saudi-led coalitions. Some Indonesian peacekeeping contributions support Gulf region stability.

financial.summary: Arabia Saudita's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has expressed interest in Indonesian infrastructure, Nusantara new capital development, and downstream nickel. Saudi Aramco is a potential partner for Indonesia's refinery expansion (Pertamina). Significant remittance flows from Indonesian workers in Arabia Saudita.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita is a key crude oil supplier to Indonesia. Saudi Aramco has discussed acquiring stakes in Indonesian refineries. Cooperation on downstream oil refining and petrochemicals is under discussion.

scientific.summary: Limited formal scientific cooperation. Some collaboration in Islamic studies and education. Saudi scholarship programmes bring Indonesian students to Saudi universities.
